_spec: BANZA QR Payload Format Specification v1.0

overview.description: The BANZA QR payload is the string encoded into a QR code image and scanned by a consumer device. It encodes enough information to initiate a payment without requiring the scanning device to be online, while keeping the payload compact for high-contrast QR rendering.

prefix_rule.description: Every BANZA QR payload MUST begin with an environment-specific prefix followed by ':' and the encoded payload body. A scanner MUST reject any string that does not begin with a recognized BANZA prefix.

production.note: Used for real payments against live wallets.

sandbox.note: Used in development and conformance testing. A payment against a BANZA-SBX: payload MUST NOT move real funds.

prefix_rule.invariant: INV-QR-ENV-001: An operator with simulated=true MUST NOT emit payloads with the BANZA: (production) prefix. This is a certification FAIL.

encoding.description: The payload body (after the prefix and ':') is Base64url-encoded JSON (RFC 4648 §5, no padding). Base64url uses '-' and '_' instead of '+' and '/', and omits trailing '=' padding.
json_rules: Keys are short single-character codes to minimize QR code density

static.description: Static QR code. Encodes the owner identity and currency. Reusable — never expires. The payer enters the amount at scan time.

oid.description: Owner ID — the UUID of the consumer or merchant wallet that receives payment.

dynamic.description: Dynamic QR code. Single-use. Amount is pre-set by the merchant. Expiry is mandatory. The payload is HMAC-signed to prevent forgery of the QR code ID or amount.

id.description: QR code ID — the UUID of the QrCode record in the operator's database. Used to fetch amount, expiry, and status on scan.

sig.description: HMAC-SHA256 signature of the signing message (Base64url, no padding). See signing_algorithm below.

signing_algorithm.purpose: Prevents forgery — an attacker cannot construct a valid dynamic QR for a different amount or recipient without the signing key.
signing_algorithm.key_source: QR_SIGNING_KEY environment variable on the operator server. Never embedded in the QR payload or exposed to clients.

signing_message.description: Pipe-delimited concatenation of the QR record fields. All fields are taken from the QrCode database record, not from the encoded payload. amount_minor is a decimal integer string. expires_at is a decimal Unix seconds string.

steps: Decode the payload to extract 'id' and 'sig'
Fetch the QrCode record from the database using 'id'
Reconstruct the signing message from the fetched record
Compare expected_sig to the 'sig' field using constant-time comparison
Check that the QrCode record status is ACTIVE
Proceed with payment only if all checks pass
verification.note: Structural decode of the payload (extracting 'id') is safe without database access. Full verification REQUIRES fetching the database record — the payload alone is insufficient.

qr_code_record.description: The server-side QrCode record that backs a dynamic QR payload. Not encoded in the QR image for dynamic QR — only the ID and signature are.

sandbox_debug_format.description: The reference sandbox operator uses an extended debug format for development convenience. This format is NOT the canonical protocol format and MUST NOT be used in production.
sandbox_debug_format.note: This format is acceptable only when simulated=true. Conformance suite vectors targeting sandbox operators accept this format. Operators targeting production MUST use the canonical compact format.

invariants.INV-QR-001: A dynamic QR code MUST be used at most once. After one successful payment, status MUST transition to USED and no further payments are accepted.
invariants.INV-QR-002: A dynamic QR code MUST have expires_at set. Static QR codes MUST have expires_at = null.
invariants.INV-QR-003: A QR code with status EXPIRED or USED MUST reject payment with an appropriate error.
invariants.INV-QR-ENV-001: An operator with simulated=true MUST emit BANZA-SBX: prefix payloads. Use of BANZA: (production prefix) in sandbox is a certification FAIL.
invariants.INV-QR-SIGN-001: The 'sig' field in dynamic QR payloads MUST be verified against the server-side QrCode record before any payment is initiated. Client-only signature validation without database record verification is insufficient.

conformance_requirements: Operator MUST accept POST /qr with { merchant_wallet_id, amount_minor?, currency, description? }
Operator MUST return { id, merchant_wallet_id, amount_minor, currency, payload_data, status, created_at }
payload_data MUST start with BANZA-SBX: (sandbox) or BANZA: (production)
Operator MUST accept POST /qr/{id}/pay with { consumer_wallet_id, amount_minor? }
A second POST /qr/{id}/pay on a USED QR MUST return HTTP 422
An open-amount QR (amount_minor=null) MUST require amount_minor in the pay request
A fixed-amount QR MUST ignore amount_minor in the pay request (use pre-set amount)
